failure_modes.text: Teams waste effort when they launch analysis or execution without agreeing on the starting condition, target outcome, or frame for comparison.
failure_modes.source_quote: Successful outcomes are predicated on defining a clear, accurate starting point.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate goal-setting from solution design: define the starting condition, target, and comparison frame as distinct steps before any analysis begins.

failure_modes.text: Improvement claims become impressionistic when normal, broken, and improved were never defined up front.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best used when later intervention quality will be judged by change from an explicit starting condition rather than by impressionistic anecdotes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Spell out all assumptions using 'what would you have to believe?' and define measurable thresholds for normal, broken, and improved before intervening.

failure_modes.text: A baseline can mislead once it fossilizes into doctrine or tracks a convenient metric that is weakly tied to the real driver of success.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the baseline against edge cases and revisit it periodically; ask whether the tracked metric still causally drives the outcome or has become a vanity measure.

premortem_questions.text: What exactly is our starting condition, and what evidence says that starting picture is accurate enough to compare against later?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Baseline Establishment is clarity of the starting condition.

premortem_questions.text: Are we defining the outcome first, or are we already drifting into execution and solution design?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Goal setting must be done without thinking about solutions or diagnosis.

premortem_questions.text: Which baseline metric is easiest to measure but least tied to the real causal driver we care about?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

heuristics.text: Define the starting picture and success criterion before expanding the work.
heuristics.source_quote: All the successful execution in the world is worthless if it’s not in service of the right outcome.

heuristics.text: Use the baseline as a comparison anchor, but keep checking whether the environment has shifted enough to require a reset.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine and the team keeps optimizing around an outdated starting frame.

heuristics.text: Do not confuse having a metric with having the right baseline.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks on starting-condition clarity, outcome-first framing, and anti-fossilized-baseline discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader prompt-design and persona-design sprawl beyond the clearest baseline moves.
open_questions: The source supports simulation and communication uses, but the Wave 2 surface stays on planning, diagnosis, and measurement baselines.

allies: The raw source is strongest alongside MECE, systems mapping, and first-principles-thinking.
antagonists: The source pushes hardest against status-quo-bias, confirmation-bias, and representativeness-bias when they harden a false baseline.
structured_tensions: The clearest tensions are with status-quo-bias, representativeness-bias, and confirmation-bias.
